tu.prop: Whole-poem fallback because Chinese and English stanza structures do not align safely.

tuv.seg: 采采卷耳，不盈頃筐，嗟我懷人，寘彼周行。 陟彼崔嵬，我馬虺隤，我姑酌彼金罍，維以不永懷。 陟彼高岡，我馬玄黃，我姑酌彼兕觥，維以不永傷。 陟彼砠矣，我馬瘏矣，我僕痡矣，云何吁矣。

I was gathering and gathering the mouse-ear, But could not fill my shallow basket. With a sigh for the man of my heart, I placed it on the high road.
I was ascending that rock-covered height, But my horses were too tired to breast it. I will now pour a cup from that gilded vase, Hoping I may not have to think of him long.
I was ascending that lofty ridge, But my horses turned of a dark yellow. I will now take a cup from that rhinoceros-horn cup, Hoping I may not have long to sorrow.
I was ascending that flat-topped height, But my horses became quite disabled, And my servants were also disabled. Oh! how great is my sorrow!
